notes: Seasonal rules (flu, COVID) use schedule+season for campaign-window suppression. One-off lifetime rules (pneumococcal, shingles, RSV) use schedule:once. INTENTIONAL OMISSIONS: pertussis-in-pregnancy and RSV-in-pregnancy are not encoded — the engine has no per-pregnancy episode gate or gestational-age gate; a lifetime lookback cannot distinguish doses given in prior pregnancies.

rules.source: JCVI/UKHSA Green Book Chapter 19; national flu immunisation programme letter 2026/27 (cohorts unchanged from 2025/26)
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear. Asthma sub-criterion requires inhaled/systemic steroids or prior hospital admission (proxy via medication check). Carers, care home residents, and household/close contacts of immunocompromised people are eligible cohorts that CANNOT be detected from a single patient's record (household composition and carer status are not reliably coded) — check these manually. BMI reading may be out of date.

rules.source: JCVI COVID-19 eligibility — autumn 2025 statement, confirmed unchanged for autumn 2026/spring 2027 by JCVI statement of 16 July 2025
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: As of 2025/26, clinical risk groups (diabetes, CHD, CKD, asthma) are NO LONGER eligible for COVID booster — only age 75+, care home residents, and immunosuppressed. Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere may not appear.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 25 (Pneumococcal, updated 12 June 2025) — single dose at age 65+; routine adult programme transitioned from PPV23 to PCV20 (Prevenar 20) in early 2026 (GOV.UK change-of-vaccine letter); existing PPV23 recipients do NOT need PCV20
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: One-off dose from age 65 (PPV23 historically, PCV20/Prevenar 20 from early 2026). Status inferred from coded records only — vaccination given elsewhere (or pre-registration) may not appear. Revaccination every 5 years applies ONLY to asplenia/splenic dysfunction/CKD and is NOT encoded here — check manually for those patients. At-risk under-65 cohort (Green Book ch 25 risk groups) intentionally not encoded.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 28a (Shingles) — phased programme from 1 Sept 2023: routine offer on turning 65 (cohort born on/after 1 Sept 1958) plus existing 70–79 cohort; Shingrix 2 doses 6–12 months apart
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY AND DOSES: Shingrix is a 2-dose course (6–12 months apart) — this chip shows GIVEN on ANY recorded shingles/zoster vaccination and CANNOT verify 2-dose completion; check dose count manually. People aged 68–69 who turned 65 before 1 Sept 2023 are NOT yet eligible and are correctly not flagged. Severely immunosuppressed patients are eligible from age 18 (lowered from 50 with effect from 1 Sept 2025) — this pathway is NOT encoded here because the engine cannot combine an age floor with clinical criteria in one clause; assess immunosuppressed patients manually. Historic Zostavax counts as given.

rules.source: UKHSA Green Book Chapter 27a (RSV) / JCVI advice June 2023; programme expanded from 1 April 2026 to all adults aged 75+ (no upper age limit) and care-home residents of any age — GOV.UK 'Expansion of RSV vaccine eligibility to those aged 80 years and older and residents in care homes for older adults' letter
rules.notes: DOUBLE-CHECK ELIGIBILITY: Single dose, no booster currently recommended. From 1 April 2026 the programme covers (a) all adults aged 75 and over who have not previously had an NHS RSV dose — the earlier 80+ exclusion is superseded — and (b) all residents in care homes for older adults, of any age. Status inferred from coded records only. RSV-in-pregnancy pathway (from 28 weeks) is intentionally NOT encoded — see pertussis omission note.
